title: Logistics Coordinator/ Delivery Driver
description:
Work Schedule: This position operates on a Tuesday to Saturday schedule, with flexibility for weekends or after-hours work when required.

JOB PURPOSE

Plan, coordinate, and manage the logistical functions (including driving-related duties) of the department in relation to roadshows, mobile blood drives, outreach events, as well as manage the store and logistical resources/assets of the department.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Logistical Management & Coordination

• Coordinate daily logistics activities, including delivery schedules, routing, and maintenance of the vehicle;

• Coordinate and manage the delivery of materials and logistical support for the publicity and promotion of mobile blood drives, road shows, deliveries to bloodbanks and events;

• Liaise with internal teams and external stakeholders to ensure smooth delivery operations;

• Monitor delivery timelines and address delays or operational issues promptly;

• Support planning and setup for events, operations, or on-site activities (where applicable).

Delivery & Driving

• Perform delivery and collection of items according to assigned routes and schedules;

• Ensure items/goods are handled, loaded, and unloaded safely and securely;

• Maintain vehicle cleanliness and report any faults or incidents promptly;

• Adhere strictly to traffic rules, safety guidelines, and organisational SOPs.

Store and Inventory Management

• Maintain the stock levels of materials used for publicity and promotion of the blood donor programme;

• Maintain the cleanliness of the store;

• Assist in packing, unpacking, and storage of logistics items;

• Undertake inventory management and tracking of all stock held in the store and bloodbanks;

• Prepare and check delivery orders, documentation, and inventory records;

• Ensure accurate tracking of items issued and returned;

• Perform basic stock counts and update records when required.

Any Other Duties

• Maintain the functionality and cleanliness of the vehicle;

• Assist with ad-hoc logistics duties as assigned;

• Support weekend or after-hours operations when required;

• Comply with workplace safety, security, and operational procedures.

JOB SPECIFICATION

Qualifications:

● Possess Nitec or Trade qualification or “O Levels” and valid Class 3 licence with good driving record.

Experience:

● 3 to 5 years of relevant experience;

● Prior experience in logistics, delivery, or operations support preferred;

● Candidate(s) with more experience may be considered as Senior Logistics Coordinator/ Delivery Driver.

Skills and knowledge

● Demonstrated interest in the non-profit/charity sector;

● Think on the feet, make decisions and carry out responsibilities without the need for direct supervision;

● Ability to follow instructions and standard operating procedures;

● Able to safely carry, move, and set up equipment required for operational duties;

● Self-motivated, responsible, adopts a proactive approach to every aspect of work;

● Good driving skills with good knowledge of road directions and public locations;

● Good communication and teamwork skills;

● Able to work independently and as part of a team;

● Good interpersonal skills, with the ability to relate well to people of all levels;

● Ability to manage store inventory and provide relevant logistical support as required;

● Basic record-keeping and documentation skills;

● Willing to perform hands-on and operational tasks;

● Willing to work on weekends/public holidays/outside office hours as and when required.

postedCompany.description:
Mission & Vision The Singapore Red Cross (SRC) was founded on 30 September 1949 and was subsequently incorporated by an Act of Parliament on 6 April 1973.

It is an independent humanitarian organisation that is non-political, non-religious and is part of the International Federation of the Red Cross.

SRC adheres to the the Geneva Convention (1949) and bases its work on the fundamental principles of humanity, impartiality, independence, neutrality, unity, universality and voluntary service.


Objectives


The objectives of the Society are able to provide assistance in relief operations in times of disaster and in auxiliary health and welfare services to the sick, the handicapped, the aged and the poor without any distinction on grounds of race, nationality, religion or political opinions, and to furnish voluntary aid to the sick and wounded in time of war and to non-belligerents, prisoners of war and civilians from the effects of war.
postedCompany.name: SINGAPORE RED CROSS SOCIETY
